answer: Implement a bottom-up mergesort with in-place merge using the block rotation algorithm. Key technical correction: O(n log² n), NOT O(n log n) - each of O(log n) merge passes requires O(n) element moves due to rotations. True O(n log n) in-place stable merge without any extra buffer remains an open problem in computer science. Algorithm: detect sorted runs, merge adjacent runs via rotations to place smaller elements first, double run size each pass. For stability: use <= comparison so equal elements from left block are placed first, preserving original relative order.
